questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: Which four of the five pieces below can be fitted together to from a perfect square?
options: ABCD
BCDE
ABCE
CDEA
questions.solution:
A square is defined as a plane figure with four equal straight sides and four right angles. Thus, when the pieces are rearranged, they should form right angle at the corner.
Above image shows how parts B, C, D, and E join together to make a perfect square.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.solution: Matching triangles in the same position of the first three hexagons cancel each other out. If all three triangles match, then the position in the fourth hexagon is a blank.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: In the following question, select that combination Of parts (A), (B), (C), (D), and (E) which is fitted together will form a square.
options: ACD
ABD
BCD
CDE
questions.solution:
Given figures:
We observe from the above figure that if we rotate (A) to the left side, and put it in the frame at the right corner of the bottom of the frame. Then, take (C) and rotate it \(360^{\circ}\), and put it left side of (A). Now take (D) and rotate it according to the shape which is left after the (A), (C). So, the final result and square created by \((\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{C}, \mathrm{D})\) is as follows,
Hence, the correct answer is ACD.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Which design should replace the question mark
?
questions.solution: The large half-circle moves from right to top to left to bottom. The medium half-circle alternates from left to right. The small halfcircle moves from bottom to right to top to left. The center dot alternates between appearing and disappearing.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
